content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 25, 2006

Stephen J. Page, MBA, OD
1113 Hopkins Way
Pleasanton, CA 94566

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-06-092

Dear Dr. Page:

This letter is in response to your request regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

You have advised that with the benefit of your "years of insight, knowledge, and investigative effort of the UC system policies and procedures" you have honed the expertise to provide demonstrable patterns of dysfunction within the UC system. The Commission may decline to provide written advice where the requestor is seeking assistance that requires us to evaluate conduct that has already occurred. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) In addition, the Commission may also decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F) – copy attached.)

Broad enforcement action against the UC system such as you describe, would be under the purview of the locally designated official, who has the primary responsibility to receive reports of allegations of suspected improper governmental activities. I have enclosed a copy of the whistleblower policy for the UC system for your review. If you believe a violation of the Act has occurred, you may also file a complaint with the Commission's Enforcement Division.

Should you have any additional questions, you may contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Joan Giannetta
Legal Assistant, Legal Division

Enclosures

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC provide written advice regarding patterns of dysfunction within the UC system based on the requestor's expertise and investigative work?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the request seeks evaluation of conduct that has already occurred, and providing such advice would be inappropriate and not in the public interest. The requestor should contact the UC system's designated whistleblower official or file a complaint with the FPPC's Enforcement Division.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter lacking formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The document presents the requestor's situation and the FPPC's reasoning for declining to provide advice. References to specific regulations (18329(b)(8)(A) and 18329(b)(8)(F)) are provided. No verbatim Q/C sections found.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can the FPPC provide written advice regarding patterns of dysfunction within the UC system based on the requestor's expertise and investigative work?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the request seeks evaluation of conduct that has already occurred, and providing such advice would be inappropriate and not in the public interest. The requestor should contact the UC system's designated whistleblower official or file a complaint with the FPPC's Enforcement Division.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice to Dr. Stephen J. Page regarding alleged patterns of dysfunction within the UC system, citing regulations that allow declination when evaluating past conduct or when advice would be inappropriate or against public interest.
